$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 13 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The guard of honour was Senna's idea of a weekend chore: he led it at San Marino for Alberti, saluted with genuine grace, and then took the weekend apart as though nothing sentimental had happened. The MADONNA 456 ran its final Gen-4B year like a swan song nobody in the garage would admit to, the PALM 190 V10 immaculate, Asselin immaculate beside it. When the Gen-5 drawings arrived mid-season the house split its drawing office without a raised voice. The veterans' autumn was everyone else's emotion. The crown's emotion, as ever, was arithmetic.
team.madonna.objective: Salute the autumn, win the present, and master Gen-5 before anyone has read it.
team.madonna.definingEvent: Senna leads the guard of honour at San Marino, then dismantles the weekend as usual.
team.madonna.carryForward: The jewel formula arrives next, and the benchmark means to greet it from in front of it.

team.firenze.summary: The ice spent the autumn in its native silence, assembling, the paddock slowly realized, one last alpine campaign. Elssler's FIRENZE 500 gave him his coldest season in years, every stint a proof that the craft era was always his country, the FIRENZE 99 V12 nursed like a family heirloom. Germi gave Italy its usual opera and found himself upstaged by the Jubilee's sentiment, which he bore with theatrical grace. The drawing office split in two for Gen-5, and the wine-and-gold jewel drawings were, by all accounts, gallery pieces. Second to the crown, and circling.
team.firenze.objective: Assemble one last alpine campaign beneath the cover of everyone else's sentiment.
team.firenze.definingEvent: The paddock notices, too late, that Elssler's coldest season in years has a shape to it.
team.firenze.carryForward: The ice enters the jewel era armed, silent, and closer than the grandees believe.

team.millions.summary: Sixteen rounds of bunting, and the patrician answered every one of them like a man insulted by the word testimonial. Alberti drove the sharpest season the #6 had given Millions in years, fast, haughty and pointedly un-retired, and at Italy the garage rolled the MILLIONS 189 out in his family's ancient colours, the richest team on the grid spending on sentiment for once. Jones kept the volume at a respectful eight. The DICK MD V10 sang its Gen-4B swan song with full chrome dignity. The Jubilee asked for gratitude; the garage gave it defiance, and everyone agreed that was better.
team.millions.objective: Honour the patrician at all sixteen rounds, and make every tribute awkward by racing under it.
team.millions.definingEvent: The #6 runs in the Alberti family's ancient colours at Italy, and the peninsula rises.
team.millions.carryForward: The autumn ends, the invoice for the jewel era arrives, and the gold intends to pay it loudly.

team.bestowal.summary: The cathedral's sleeves were already rolled when Gen-5 was unveiled, exactly as planned. Bestowal's season in the present was dignified sufficiency, Blume correct, Picos warm, the BESTOWAL 167 banking its patrician haul, while the real work happened on the second drawing board, where the benefactor's hoarded budget bought the grid's most advanced jewel programme. The VAPOR DN's swan song was played at the cathedrals only. The mood in the marble garage was the confidence of people who had seen the future's invoice and already paid it.
team.bestowal.objective: Bank the present quietly and win the winter: the most advanced Gen-5 programme on the grid.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: Gen-5 is unveiled at West Germany, and the cathedral's second drawing board is already ahead.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The jewel formula opens next, and German steel intends to be its reference from the first lap.

team.blanche.summary: The white house had two autumns at once. Herbin spent his proving the Charter era belonged to the craftsmen now, the BLANCHE 582's DELTA 103 V10 thrift and precision the midfield's standing reference, the aristocrats studying his laps like apprentices. And at a wet, grey Great Britain, White had his second sunrise: the one-win believer ran the rain like scripture, the white car spotless in the spray, and the grandstand adopted him for good whatever the flag finally said. The whitecoats, who do not do sentiment, were observed to do something very near it. Both autumns were earned.
team.blanche.objective: Prove the era belongs to the craftsmen, and give the believer his day in the rain.
team.blanche.definingEvent: White's wet Great Britain afternoon, the second sunrise, and the grandstand adopting him for good.
team.blanche.carryForward: The white car enters the jewel era as the reference every drawing office is already copying.

team.tyrant.summary: The boot camp enjoyed the sentimental year in its own way: by refusing to be sentimental. Hamano's crossover discipline kept the TYRANT 548 at LEVEL B's front, the LIZZIE 24 V8 a byword for reliability, and at Japan the home crowd got its annual lesson in patience. Gould supplied the emotion the garage declined to, the island's late-braking sermon delivered on schedule at Canada. When Gen-5 was unveiled the gunmetal men split the shop in two without ceremony. The veterans could have their autumn. Tyrant was already working on spring.
team.tyrant.objective: Hold the LEVEL B front through the autumn, and be early to the jewel formula's door.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Hamano's home discipline silences the Japan crowd into applause, again.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The jewel era will reward the exact virtues the boot camp drills, and the blades are ready.

team.losel.summary: The two-programme season was Christmas for the data door: Losel had modelled the new formula's first principles a year early, and when Gen-5 was unveiled the slide-rule garage was the only one in the paddock that did not flinch. Pacheco spent the present proving the Charter era belonged to its craftsmen, the LOSEL 125's VAPOR DNPQ V8 closing its Gen-4B account in credit, Dehehe ticking in support. The veterans' autumn got its tribute from Losel in the only currency the garage trades: a bound dossier on how the jewel formula would behave. It was, as usual, right.
team.losel.objective: Be the only garage that does not flinch when the jewel formula is read aloud.
team.losel.definingEvent: The Gen-5 dossier, written a year early, matches the unveiled formula line for line.
team.losel.carryForward: The data door enters the jewel era with a year's head start and no intention of admitting it.

team.may.summary: The young face in a sentimental year. While the paddock polished its heritage, the leaf-green garage simply bloomed on schedule, Turner waving at grandstands that had come to remember and finding he preferred making them forget. The MAY 555's LORRY 32 V8 completed another loyal campaign, and the slow-corner traction bought last winter kept the #15 honest in the midfield mugging trade. Gen-5's unveiling worried the little teams until the customer-engine promise was read aloud, at which point the crew whistled again. Spring, the garage noted, does not do autumn. It does next.
team.may.objective: Be the season's young face, and let the veterans have the memories.
team.may.definingEvent: The customer-engine promise is read aloud, and the little garage whistles again.
team.may.carryForward: The jewel formula promises the little teams finishing power, and May means to spend it on attack.

team.bullets.summary: On the eve of West Germany, the insurgent heard the future read aloud: a jewel formula, safety-built and close-racing, and, the sentence that changed everything in the lock-up, customer engines that finished. The plan that had been forming for two years crystallized overnight. Ceara ran the featherweight BULLETS 560 through Gen-4B's last autumn with something new in his driving: economy, a man saving himself, and the hand-fettled LIZZIE 24 V8 kept its temper. The veterans could have their autumn of gratitude. Down in the backstreet, the ledger of things to sell was being priced, item by item.
team.bullets.objective: Save the fire, price the garage, and be ready when the customer engines arrive.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The Gen-5 announcement names customer engines, and the lock-up's plan becomes a decision.
team.bullets.carryForward: The second wind is coming with the jewel formula, and the garage has already sold its first item.

team.dardan.summary: The homecoming. At Italy, in the same weekend the Jubilee painted the peninsula sentimental, Bellini brought the oxblood spear home, and discovered, to everyone's astonishment including his own, that the tifosi had been waiting for him. The cheer that met the DARDAN 700 at the temple was a decade of grudge exhaling at once. Alfven, ice over oxblood, banked the season's quiet arithmetic beside him, the VAPOR DNPQ V8 faultless. The old spear wept, the garage pretended he had not, and the clan stood taller than it had since the protest years. The feud slept on. Something else had woken.
team.dardan.objective: Bring the spear home to Italy, and find out what a decade of exile was actually for.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The tifosi receive Bellini at Italy like a son, and the oxblood garage exhales.
team.dardan.carryForward: The grudge has one chapter left in it, and it will be written with an open hand.

team.linden.summary: Magnificently, simply, still there. Moreau marked the veterans' autumn his way: no ceremony, one nod to the old linden-green car, and straight back to work, adding another season to the longest single-team tenure the championship has ever kept. The LINDEN LN198 finished with its usual agrarian patience, the LORRY 32 V8 sipping where others gulped, and when Gen-5 was unveiled the oldest workshop on the grid began, unhurriedly, to season its third generation of machinery. Asked about the autumn, the paddock's philosopher called it another Tuesday with a nicer crowd. The watch kept being set.
team.linden.objective: Outlast a third generation of machinery, and mark it with work rather than ceremony.
team.linden.definingEvent: One nod to the old green car, and straight back to work, the autumn's shortest speech.
team.linden.carryForward: The jewel era gets the same elder the iron era got: patient, present, and impossible to move.

team.minarae.summary: The forge spent the veterans' autumn stoking. Nono's consolidation became genuine menace at the front of LEVEL C, the #21 now the first name the big teams circled on Friday, and Miller spent the year drilling him like a man passing on everything before it stopped mattering. The MINARAE 594 and SEGA SG1000 V8 closed Gen-4B's account strongly. When Gen-5 was unveiled, with customer engines that finished, the whole garage understood at once what it meant: the gap that had always been machinery was about to become merely money, and money, Minarae had learned, could be borrowed against belief.
team.minarae.objective: Turn the heir's menace into the grid's worst Friday habit, and ready the forge for Gen-5.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Nono becomes the first name the big teams circle on a Friday.
team.minarae.carryForward: The jewel era will hand the forge finishing power, and the flame is one winter from lit.

team.rigel.summary: The museum faced the future's unveiling with a restorer's calm: Gen-5 meant new drawings, and new drawings meant the lifers would finally learn whether they were builders as well as curators. The present went quietly, Cotman and Chardin nursing the RIGEL 3000 through Gen-4B's last autumn, the LORRY 32 V8 restored one final time to something like its youth. Cotman banked finishes; Chardin held doors. The veterans' autumn suited the garage's whole temperament, and the trophy shelf got an extra dusting for the occasion. The starlight, the lifers agreed, was worth following one formula more.
team.rigel.objective: Close the Gen-4B account with dignity, and dare to be builders in the next one.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The museum resolves to design its first new jewel rather than restore an old one.
team.rigel.carryForward: The jewel era will test the restorers' one original idea, and they are ready to be measured.

team.comet.summary: One of the two circled grey afternoons nearly paid. Tornio read a sky the whole pit lane misread, coaxed the COMET 323 into a position the black car had no business holding, and held it until the light and the rubber ran out together. The garage called it the best nearly in years, and meant it warmly. The LIZZIE 24 V8 completed another faithful campaign. The veterans' autumn was the romantics' natural season, and the old highlight reel got an extra airing in the clubhouse. The pan stayed cold. The faith, warmed by the nearly, did not.
team.comet.objective: Cash one circled afternoon, and let the veterans' autumn warm the old reel.
team.comet.definingEvent: The best nearly in years: Tornio holds a stolen position until the light runs out.
team.comet.carryForward: The jewel era promises finishing machinery, and the romantics want one more blaze before the end.

team.orchis.summary: The gardener-principal spent the veterans' autumn doing what glasshouses do in autumn: pruning for next year. The ORCHIS 056's MISFIRE 50 V8 completed a third clean season, the survival-first doctrine banked its modest cool-weather harvests, and Tegner, the frost gambler turned professor of endurance, began teaching the car to live on less. Gen-5's unveiling, with customer engines that finished, was read twice in the little greenhouse. A flower that survives everything, the gardener noted, could bloom anywhere. The scraps of the family war had been cultivated. The frost gambler checked the sky, and planted for spring.
team.orchis.objective: Prune, endure, and plant the survival doctrine into the jewel era.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The customer-engine promise reaches the greenhouse, and the gardener plants for spring.
team.orchis.carryForward: A flower that survives everything can bloom anywhere, and the jewel era will be asked to prove it.

team.zeroforce.summary: The date arrived. When the governing office unveiled Gen-5 and read the words customer engine programme aloud, the last garage in the pit lane allowed itself one second of silence before going back to work. Klinger spent Gen-4B's last autumn preparing the ZEROFORCE 231 for machinery that might finally finish what he started, and the old LORRY 32 V8 gave its final account with dignity. The chalk wall recorded another honest year of arithmetic. The gallows humour acquired a new edge, dangerous, unfamiliar: hope. The queue for the new engines formed, and Zeroforce was first in it.
team.zeroforce.objective: Be first in the customer-engine queue, and live to see the patience cashed.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The customer engine programme is read aloud, and the floor goes silent for one second.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: Next season the car can finish what the man starts, and the whole pit lane knows his name.

team.joke.summary: The carnival spent the veterans' autumn lampooning ceremony itself, which is the highest form of respect the Joke garage knows. Dufay attended every Jubilee tribute with a straight face, an achievement in itself, and answered the season's one question about his own future with a silence so theatrical the press corps wrote it down. The JOKE 777's POND V8 ran thriftily, the #16's fake-strategy theatre reached new audiences, and the ledger held in LEVEL B. Gen-5 was unveiled, and the carnival crew immediately began designing fake heritage for it. Some garages honour history. This one rents it.
team.joke.objective: Lampoon the autumn's ceremony lovingly, and keep the LEVEL B ledger honest.
team.joke.definingEvent: Dufay's theatrical silence about his own future becomes the press's favourite non-story.
team.joke.carryForward: The jewel era will be beautiful, and the carnival already has plans to be ugly in it.

team.lares.summary: The hearth's natural season. While the paddock said its thank-yous, the little house that says thank-you by keeping the lamp lit simply kept it lit, Arai bringing the LARES 92 home with the usual courteous reliability, the RAM V12's manners intact. The veterans' autumn filled the garage with visitors, old hands who had once been given their feet in the hearth-red car and came back to nod at the mantel. The kettle never went off the boil. Gen-5's unveiling was noted, discussed over supper, and greeted with the house's whole philosophy: whatever the formula, the car will come home.
team.lares.objective: Host the autumn, keep the lamp lit, and bring the car home as always.
team.lares.definingEvent: The old hands come back to nod at the mantel, and the kettle never goes off the boil.
team.lares.carryForward: The jewel era will need one garage that never changes, and the hearth volunteers.

team.feet.summary: The grafters saluted the veterans the way they salute everything: by out-working them. Rampal dragged the FEET 13 through Gen-4B's last autumn with both tools now in the box, sweat and the new speed, and Old Boots came home in the points like a man punching a clock. The YOUGEN V10's exhaust package got a final evolution and a final accounting. When Gen-5 was unveiled the workshop did not cheer or worry; it rolled up its sleeves, because a new formula means new work, and work is the one thing this garage has never once been short of.
team.feet.objective: Punch the clock through the autumn, and be first to the new formula's workbench.
team.feet.definingEvent: Gen-5 is unveiled, and the garage's only reaction is rolled-up sleeves.
team.feet.carryForward: The jewel era will be built by hand somewhere, and the grafters intend it to be their hands.

team.serga.summary: The mountain man spent the veterans' autumn being quietly claimed as one of the veterans, which embarrassed him more than any crash. Sambena welded, drove and banked another honest season, the SC3000 F12 finishing with the stubbornness its owner signs cheques with. The thousand small names on the flanks had become the paddock's favourite parable, and when Gen-5 was unveiled the grey garage was cited in speeches as proof of what the sport was for. Sambena heard the speeches, said nothing, and went back to the welding. The thanks, he noted, were already on the car.
team.serga.objective: Weld, drive, finish, and endure being the paddock's favourite parable.
team.serga.definingEvent: The grey car is cited in the Gen-5 speeches as what the sport is for.
team.serga.carryForward: The parable is about to be retold at national scale, and the original keeps welding.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs were the best seats in the house for the veterans' autumn, and Cool knew it. The garage watched a season of tributes, parades and weeping grandstands with enormous enjoyment, Delvaux chasing the light through Gen-4B's farewell, the CORSE V8 humming its sunset tune. One top ten arrived and was celebrated until the light failed, as tradition demands. Gen-5's unveiling caused a brief stir in the deckchairs, resolved when someone noted the jewel cars would be very pretty to watch go past. The big board said what it liked. The light went gold. The glass was raised.
team.cool.objective: Enjoy the autumn from the deckchairs, and raise a glass to everyone else's emotions.
team.cool.definingEvent: The Gen-5 stir is resolved by the observation that the new cars will be pretty.
team.cool.carryForward: The jewel era will be the prettiest show on earth, and Cool has the seats for it.

team.moon.summary: The telescope had been pointed at this winter for two years, and when Gen-5 was unveiled, the far side of the grid allowed itself a small, cold celebration: one extra candle in the garage, lit at dusk. Moon's present remained Moon's present, Yepes waving the leaders through, the MOON 292's RAM V12 completing Gen-4B's last autumn with the patience of orbit. The chalk said the gap was now merely a gap, and the new formula promised to close it further. Every moon is on the rise, the garage said, and for once the stars seemed to agree.
team.moon.objective: Survive the last autumn, and be lit and ready when the customer engines arrive.
team.moon.definingEvent: One extra candle is lit in the garage at dusk, the Gen-5 celebration.
team.moon.carryForward: The jewel era promises to close the gap, and the telescope is finally pointed at something real.

team.iris.summary: The vow got its second act, louder. Salgado renewed the Portugal promise in public at the spring rounds, one more crown before the fire cools, and Iris committed its whole season to the hunt, the IRIS 717 cut ever sharper, the PRISM 90 V10 singing at the razor's edge. The sister house was asked to help and pointedly declined, which the violet garage treated as fuel. The white gloves ran a campaign of total commitment and elegance, and the form book kept the flame armed all autumn. The fire had not cooled. The deadline now had a clock on it.
team.iris.objective: Commit the whole house to the vow, and race the calendar as hard as the grid.
team.iris.definingEvent: The sister house declines to help, and the violet garage converts the snub into fuel.
team.iris.carryForward: The jewel era resets every clock on the grid except one: the fire's deadline still ticks.

team.azalea.summary: The glasshouse spent the veterans' autumn proving the Charter era had a second generation, and it wore magenta. Asked to lend telemetry to the sister house's vow, Azalea pointedly declined, and spent the season defending what a decade had built: Larssen's cold arithmetic crowding the front with the risen craftsmen, the rooftop lung now a page in everyone's copybook, the BLOOM 88 V8 immaculate. The bloom, once open, did not close. The veterans could have their autumn of gratitude; the frost was having a decade of it, and intended the next era to be frosty too.
team.azalea.objective: Defend the bloom, decline the sister's vow, and prove the era belongs to the frost.
team.azalea.definingEvent: Azalea refuses Iris's telemetry request, and the family war finds its permanent border.
team.azalea.carryForward: The jewel era opens with the frost at the front, and the glasshouse intends to winter there.
